meta.title: Otoifuji Ski Area — LP Mock (Hidden Japow · Station Walk · Dual Pricing Strategy)
meta.description: Japan's lightest dry powder, 84% advanced skiers. A hidden Japow gem in Otoineppu—Hokkaido's smallest village (535 residents). 15-minute walk from JR Otoineppu Station. LP mock based on Otoifuji Visitor Pass, ¥40,000 CAT tour, FIS/SAJ certified XC, and Tomte lodging hub strategy.

banner.text: Hidden Japow · station walk · Otoifuji Visitor Pass · CAT tour strategy | AI-generated mock images

hero.title:
Otoifuji
Ski Area
hero.tagline: Japan's lightest dry Japow—84% advanced skiers, a hidden gem you can reach on foot from the station.

transit.title:
15 minutes from the station
to the snow.
transit.lead: About a 15-minute walk from JR Otoineppu Station—a rare train-accessible ski area in Hokkaido. Along National Route 40, it is a micro-tourism base where advanced Japow is within reach without a rental car. Dry powder without the crowds, in the quiet of a village of 535.
transit.imgAlt: A 15-minute walk from Otoineppu Station toward the snow—a rare train-accessible ski hill

primary.title: Lightest dry Japow · 84% advanced
primary.lead: One pair lift, three courses, 182 m vertical—compact in scale, yet Japan's lightest dry powder and an 84% advanced-skier ratio signal the real thing. A hidden gem in Otoineppu Village, off the mega-resort circuit.
primary.imgAlt: Uncrowded dry powder burn—an advanced-skier hidden Japow hill

secondary.title: Calm of Hokkaido's smallest village
secondary.lead: 535 residents—Hokkaido's smallest municipality. A remote local sanctuary with virtually no lift lines and a private-slope feel, plus the rare asset of a 15-minute station walk.

pass.title:
Villager AM ¥1,300,
Visitor ¥3,500+.
pass.lead: Keep the villager morning ticket at ¥1,300 while offering inbound and distant visitors the Otoifuji Visitor Pass (¥3,500–4,500)—a dual-pricing model that protects everyday local use and lifts Japow-experience ARPU (LP concept).
pass.noticeBody: Protect "villagers' morning" pricing and uncrowded snow quality for 84% advanced skiers, while doubling guest spend through the Visitor Pass and CAT tour—a revenue pivot for a smallest-village ski hill.
pass.imgAlt: A quiet Otoineppu slope—dual pricing that serves both locals and visitors

cat.title:
CAT tour,
¥40,000.
cat.lead: Snowcat access to untouched dry Japow—Otoifuji's flagship product. Package backcountry-grade advanced terrain as a guided, safe CAT tour. A high-value line combined with the Visitor Pass and Tomte stays (LP strategy).
cat.whisper: A flagship built on 84% advanced skiers—beyond a single pair lift, the core of differentiation and guest spend.
cat.imgAlt: Snowcat into untouched powder—¥40,000 flagship CAT tour

xc.title:
Certified XC,
5 · 7.5 · 10 km.
xc.lead: FIS/SAJ certified cross-country courses at 5 km, 7.5 km, and 10 km. A non-lift winter-sports axis that extends advanced guests' stay length and village spend beyond alpine runs.
xc.imgAlt: FIS/SAJ certified cross-country course—Nordic skiing across open snow

tomte.title:
Tomte,
80-bed hub.
tomte.lead: Tomte lodging—80-guest capacity with a wax room. A camp hub tying together CAT tours, XC, and night skiing, concentrating stay demand in the smallest village (LP concept).
tomte.noticeBody: Visitor Pass + Tomte + CAT tour bundle—a 1-night/2-day advanced Japow stay designed to lift lodging and dining ARPU in a village of 535.
tomte.imgAlt: Tomte lodge—80-bed ski camp base with wax room

ecosystem.title:
Black station soba,
Tengengawa onsen.
ecosystem.lead: About 15 minutes on foot toward the station from the hill—famous black station soba for lunch. After runs, warm up with a Tengengawa onsen package—integrating the smallest village's food-and-onsen ecosystem into the Visitor Pass.
ecosystem.sobaLead: A local specialty near Otoineppu Station—chewy black station soba. A station-walk lunch that gives train travelers one more reason to stay.
ecosystem.onsenLead: Tengengawa onsen package partnership—warm cold bodies and complete the recovery phase of a multi-night stay.
ecosystem.imgAlt: Black station soba—a famous lunch within walking distance of Otoineppu Station

journey.title:
Arrive by train,
snow in 15 minutes.
journey.lead: Take JR from Sapporo or Asahikawa to Otoineppu—an advanced Japow journey without a rental car. A half-day to 2-night model linking station walk, black station soba, Tengengawa onsen, and Tomte stays.

01.title: JR Otoineppu Station
01.body: Arrive by train. 15-minute walk to the hill

03.title: Black station soba
03.body: Famous lunch within station walking distance

05.title: Tomte overnight
05.body: 80 beds with wax room. CAT tour the next day

japow.title: Complete guide to hidden Japow—lightest dry powder
japow.body: 84% advanced skiers, one pair lift, three courses over 182 m—small in scale, yet rated for Japan's lightest dry powder. One step off the crowded mega-resort circuit, Japow with a private-slope feel in Hokkaido's smallest village of 535.

catGuide.title: ¥40,000 CAT tour—flagship product guide
catGuide.body: Snowcat into untouched zones—safety design, advanced routes, and bundling with the Visitor Pass and Tomte stays. A high-value line that goes beyond a single pair lift (LP strategy note).

transit.title: 15-minute station walk × village ecosystem model course
transit.body: Rare 15-minute walk from JR Otoineppu Station, FIS/SAJ certified XC (5/7.5/10 km), black station soba, Tengengawa onsen, Wed/Fri/Sat night skiing (17:00–20:00)—a half-day to 2-night loop tying eat, ski, and stay in a village of 535.

access.note: About 15 minutes on foot from JR Otoineppu Station · along National Route 40
